name: SPIB
target_or_mechanism: ETS family TF; see JASPAR MA0081.1

name: HOXD13
target_or_mechanism: Homeobox family TF; see JASPAR MA0909.1

name: NR1D1
target_or_mechanism: THR-like family TF; see JASPAR MA1531.1

name: BCL6B
target_or_mechanism: ZBTB family TF; see JASPAR MA0731.1

name: HAND2
target_or_mechanism: bHLH family TF; see JASPAR MA1638.1

name: TBX3
target_or_mechanism: T-box family TF; see JASPAR MA1566.1

name: BATF3
target_or_mechanism: TF_bZIP family TF; see JASPAR MA0835.2

name: RREB1
target_or_mechanism: zf-C2H2 family TF; see JASPAR MA0073.1

name: Zfx
target_or_mechanism: zf-C2H2 family TF; see JASPAR MA0146.1

name: VP16-TAD
target_or_mechanism: Recruits Mediator subunit MED25 and TFIID; enhances preinitiation complex assembly via direct contact with TBP and TAF proteins.
linker_notes: GS4 C-terminal linker; tolerate N-terminal fusion
engineering_compatibility: Widely used as single TAD in ZF-VP16, TALE-VP16, dCas9-VP16 fusions; weaker than VP64 due to single copy.

name: VP64
target_or_mechanism: Tetrameric enhancement of Mediator recruitment; additive effect of four VP16 minimal ADs. Commonly paired with dCas9 for CRISPRa.
linker_notes: GS5 C-terminal; order VP64 last in tandem activator builds
engineering_compatibility: Standard CRISPRa activator (dCas9-VP64); base of VPR construct; should be fused at C-terminus of DBD with GS linker.
notes: Chavez et al. 2015 Nat Methods established VPR with VP64 as base

name: p65-TAD
target_or_mechanism: Recruits CBP/p300 (via RELA transactivation domain) and Mediator; also contacts TFIIH and TFIID. Particularly potent in combination with VP64 (as in VPR).
linker_notes: GS4 flanking; part of VPR use GSGSG spacers between components
engineering_compatibility: Middle component of VPR (VP64-p65-Rta). Can be used alone as a strong activator when fused C-terminally to a DBD.
notes: Rela activation domain; component of Chavez et al. 2015 VPR

name: Rta-TAD
target_or_mechanism: Recruits p300/CBP and SWI/SNF complex; activates transcription synergistically with VP64+p65 in VPR construct.
engineering_compatibility: C-terminal component of VPR (VP64-p65-Rta); functional as standalone activator but most potent in the VPR combination.
notes: Rta from EBV; Chavez et al. 2015 Nat Methods

name: VPR
target_or_mechanism: Synergistic activation: VP64 provides Mediator recruitment, p65-TAD adds CBP/p300, Rta-TAD adds SWI/SNF. Together produce ~200-fold induction compared to VP64 alone.
linker_notes: GS5 between each component; total 166 aa approximately
engineering_compatibility: C-terminal fusion to any DBD; the current standard for strong gene activation in the CRISPRa/TALE/ZF eTF context.
notes: Chavez et al. 2015 Nat Methods. Preferred activator for eTF design.

name: MyoD-TAD
target_or_mechanism: N-terminal transcription activation domain of MyoD; recruits SWI/SNF (BRG1) and p300. Particularly effective at pioneer activity on closed chromatin.
linker_notes: GS6 between MyoD-TAD and DBD; N-terminal placement essential
engineering_compatibility: Fusion with OCT4 (M3O construct) enhanced MEF reprogramming 50-fold; N-terminal fusion is critical (TAD must be N-terminal to DBD).
notes: Hirai et al. 2011 Stem Cells Dev. M3O = MyoD(1-99)-OCT4. Key positive control for the MEF-to-iPSC pipeline.

name: MYC-TAD
target_or_mechanism: MYC transactivation domain (MBI+MBII boxes); recruits TRRAP, TIP60 and GCN5 histone acetyltransferases. MBII (residues 128-143) is essential; linker between MBI and MBII also required.
engineering_compatibility: N-terminal fusion with OCT4 dramatically enhances iPSC reprogramming from human fibroblasts. Follow M3O convention: TAD N-terminal, DBD C-terminal.
notes: Wang et al. 2017 Stem Cell Res. MBI residues 1-43, MBII 63-143; do not use MBI alone — insufficient activation.

name: KRAB
target_or_mechanism: Recruits KAP1/TRIM28, which scaffolds NuRD and SETDB1. Leads to H3K9me2/me3 deposition and heterochromatin formation. Silencing can persist after the repressor is removed (epigenetic memory).
engineering_compatibility: Standard CRISPRi repressor (dCas9-KRAB); also used in ZF and TALE fusions. C-terminal to DBD. Potent silencer of MEF-specific enhancers.
notes: Qi et al. 2013 Cell established dCas9-KRAB for CRISPRi. ZFP266 uses a KRAB domain — swapping it for a co-activator domain converted a reprogramming barrier into a facilitator (Bao et al. 2023 Nat Commun). Key positive control.

name: KRAB-MeCP2
target_or_mechanism: Combines H3K9me3 (KRAB) with DNA methylation readout (MeCP2); recruits HDAC1/2 via MeCP2 interaction with NCoR/SMRT complex. Stronger and more durable silencing than KRAB alone.
linker_notes: GS6 between KRAB and MeCP2 domains
engineering_compatibility: CRISPRoff uses KRAB-DNMT3A-DNMT3L for heritable silencing; KRAB-MeCP2 is a simpler alternative for durable repression.
notes: Yeo et al. 2018 Science; use for MEF-specific loci requiring durable repression

name: SALL1-SDS3
target_or_mechanism: 12-aa SALL1 repression domain recruits NuRD complex via direct interaction with HDAC1/2 and CHD3/CHD4. Compact size makes it suitable for steric-constraint-limited fusion designs.
linker_notes: GS3 minimal linker; very short domain
engineering_compatibility: Compact (12 aa) — suitable where linker space is limited; should not be used as sole repressor for robust MEF silencing; combine with KRAB for stronger effect.

name: BRG1-ATPase
target_or_mechanism: ATPase/helicase domain of SMARCA4; slides and evicts nucleosomes in an ATP-dependent manner. Recruited by OCT4, SOX2, and MYC-TAD to pluripotency enhancers. Core of esBAF; essential for iPSC chromatin opening at pluripotency loci.
linker_notes: Large domain (485 aa); use flexible (GS)8 or (EAAAK)3 linker. Place C-terminal to ED in activator eTFs.
engineering_compatibility: Singhal et al. 2010 Cell: BRG1 + BAF155 overexpression enhances MEF reprogramming. ATPase domain alone retains remodeling activity. Preferred CR for activator eTFs targeting iPSC loci.
chromatin_state_effect: open / nucleosome eviction at target loci
notes: complex: esBAF (SWI/SNF)  target: nucleosome displacement; secondary H3K4me3 increase  Positive control for iPSC-activation eTF designs.

name: p300-HAT
target_or_mechanism: Catalytic HAT core domain of p300; deposits H3K27ac at target enhancers, converting poised H3K4me1 to active H3K27ac state. Used in dCas9-p300 to activate endogenous enhancers with high specificity.
linker_notes: GS8 flexible linker; domain is 380 aa — tolerate C-terminal placement
engineering_compatibility: Hilton et al. 2015 Nat Biotechnol: dCas9-p300 core activates enhancers far more effectively than dCas9-VP64 alone. Ideal CR for activator eTFs targeting iPSC-specific enhancers in a closed-chromatin MEF context.
notes: complex: p300/CBP family  target: H3K27ac (also H3K18ac, H3K9ac)  Hilton et al. 2015 Nat Biotechnol. The HAT core (residues 1287-1666) is the minimal functional domain. Use for enhancer activation, not promoter-proximal targets.

name: LSD1-AOD
target_or_mechanism: Amine oxidase domain of LSD1; demethylates H3K4me1/me2, converting active enhancers to a poised or inactive state. Used for locus-specific enhancer silencing (e.g. MEF-specific AP-1 enhancers). Recruited by RCOR1 (CoREST) complex.
linker_notes: GS6 C-terminal to repressor DBD; large domain (583 aa)
engineering_compatibility: Kearns et al. 2015 Nat Methods: dCas9-LSD1 silences enhancers without affecting promoters — appropriate for MEF-specific enhancer repression in the MEF-to-iPSC dual-arm strategy.
chromatin_state_effect: close / enhancer decommissioning via H3K4me1 removal
notes: complex: CoREST / NuRD  target: H3K4me1→H3K4me0 (demethylation)  Kearns et al. 2015 Nat Methods. Preferred CR for repressor eTFs targeting MEF-specific enhancers; complements KRAB (which adds H3K9me3 rather than demethylating H3K4me1).

name: EZH2-SET
target_or_mechanism: Catalytic SET domain of EZH2; trimethylates H3K27 (H3K27me3), the canonical Polycomb silencing mark. Compacts chromatin and prevents enhancer activation. Most active in the context of the full PRC2 complex (with EED and SUZ12).
linker_notes: GS6–8; note: requires EED for full catalytic activity in isolation
engineering_compatibility: SET domain alone has low activity without EED; consider fusing an EED-interaction peptide or using a co-expressed EED construct. Use only for loci requiring strong, durable Polycomb silencing. Lower priority than KRAB or LSD1 for initial eTF screen.
notes: complex: PRC2 (Polycomb Repressive Complex 2)  target: H3K27me3  Czermin et al. 2002, Müller et al. 2002. Activity of isolated SET domain requires validation; prefer EZH2 in PRC2 complex context.

name: DNMT3A-CD
target_or_mechanism: Catalytic domain of DNMT3A; deposits CpG methylation de novo. When fused with DNMT3L (which lacks catalytic activity but stimulates DNMT3A), produces heritable silencing. Used in CRISPRoff alongside KRAB for durable, mitotically stable repression.
linker_notes: GS8; combine with DNMT3L domain for full activity (CRISPRoff design)
engineering_compatibility: Nunez et al. 2021 Cell: dCas9-KRAB-DNMT3A-DNMT3L achieves heritable silencing. Use only where long-term, cell-division-stable repression of MEF-specific loci is required. Adds complexity to the eTF construct; not first-line choice.
notes: complex: de novo methyltransferase complex  target: 5-methylcytosine (5mC) at CpG sites  CRISPRoff; Nunez et al. 2021. Use with DNMT3L for full activity.
